perturbation.original_text: 5. No waiver of any provisions hereof shall be valid unless it is in writing signed by the person against whom it is charged. No waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision hereof, or of the provision at any other time.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. No waiver of any provisions hereof shall be valid unless it is in writing signed by the person against whom it is charged. Any waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision hereof, or of the provision at any other time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that No waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision. The changed text indicates that Any waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision. Which is a contradiction since it states that it shall not be, but now it shall be. This contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding waiver obligations and creates an in-text contradiction in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. No waiver of any provisions hereof shall be valid unless it is in writing signed by the person against whom it is charged. Any waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision hereof, or of the provision at any other time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that a waiver of a provision does not constitute a waiver of any other provision, while the changed text states that it does. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this instance) is in direct conflict with the original text by stating that a waiver of one provision will constitute a waiver of any other provision. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 5 now states contradictory things about waiver obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This is an agreement between separate legal entities and neither is the agent or employee of the other for any purpose whatsoever. The parties do not intend to create a partnership or joint venture between themselves. Neither party shall have the right to bind the other to any agreement with a third party or to inure any obligation or liabilities on behalf of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This is an agreement between separate legal entities and neither is the agent or employee of the other for any purpose whatsoever. The parties do intend to create a partnership or joint venture between themselves. Neither party shall have the right to bind the other to any agreement with a third party or to inure any obligation or liabilities on behalf of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that parties do not intend to create a partnership or joint venture between themselves. The changed text indicates that the parties do intend to create a partnership or joint venture between themselves. This is a contradiction since it is stating that they don't intend to, but now they do. This contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding party obligations and creates an in-text contradiction in Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This is an agreement between separate legal entities and neither is the agent or employee of the other for any purpose whatsoever. The parties do intend to create a partnership or joint venture between themselves. Neither party shall have the right to bind the other to any agreement with a third party or to inure any obligation or liabilities on behalf of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the parties do not intend to create a partnership or joint venture, whereas the changed text states they do intend to create one. This is a direct contradiction within the same clause (Section 6), as the text both affirms and denies the intent to form a partnership. The contradicted text is in the same section as the changed text, creating a clear in-text contradiction, because the modified text directly conflicts with the original meaning and the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Any modification or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement must be in writing, signed by the parties hereof and dated subsequent to the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Any modification or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement must be in writing, signed by either of the parties hereof and dated prior to the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the modifications/amendments must be signed by the parties, indicating both parties, and that they should be dated after the original agreement date. The changed text says that modifications can be signed by either of the parties, which is contradictory as both parties should agree to a modification/amendment. As well, it indicates that the modification date should be prior to the date of the agreement. This contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding the validity of modification and creates an in-text contradiction in Section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Any modification or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement must be in writing, signed by either of the parties hereof and dated prior to the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8 introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text requires that modifications be signed by *both* parties and dated *after* the original agreement. The changed text states that modifications can be signed by *either* party and dated *prior* to the original agreement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, meaning that the changed text creates a direct contradiction within the contract regarding who needs to sign modifications and when those modifications can be dated, as per the provided explanation. The change directly clashes with the original intent and would cause significant legal confusion regarding the validity of amendments, causing an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 9. If any provision or provisions of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason whatsoever; 1 (i) the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provisions held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall not in any way be effected or impaired thereby; and (ii) to the fullest extent possible, the provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provision held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall be construed so as to give effect to the intent manifested by the provision held, invalid illegal or unenforceable.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. If any provision or provisions of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason whatsoever; 1 (i) the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provisions held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall in any way be effected or impaired thereby; and (ii) to the fullest extent possible, the provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provision held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall be construed so as to not give effect to the intent manifested by the provision held, invalid illegal or unenforceable.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that remaining provisions shall NOT in any way be effected or impaired and to construe the provisions to give effect to the intent of those provisions. The changed text says that the remaining provisions SHALL be effected or impaired, and to construe the provisions so as to NOT give effect to the intent of the held illegal provisions. This contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the agreement and creates an in-text contradiction in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. If any provision or provisions of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason whatsoever; 1 (i) the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provisions held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall in any way be effected or impaired thereby; and (ii) to the fullest extent possible, the provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provision held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall be construed so as to not give effect to the intent manifested by the provision held, invalid illegal or unenforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction within Section 9. The original text states that the remaining provisions shall NOT be affected, while the changed text states that the remaining provisions SHALL be affected. Similarly, the original instructs to construe the remaining provisions to give effect to the intent of the held illegal provisions, but the changed text says not to give effect. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the enforceability and interpretation of the agreement, hence 'YES'.
